title: patch_block input schema
description: Input contract for the patch_block typed MCP tool. Authoritative source is the Zod schema in src/tools/patch_block/schema.ts; this JSON Schema is the human-readable cross-reference and the form published via zodToJsonSchema at registration time. Uses the project's target_mode discriminator per ADR-003 — in specific mode, vault is required plus one of file/path; in active mode, none of vault/file/path may appear.

target_mode.description: Locator-shape discriminator per ADR-003. 'specific' = caller supplies vault + file|path. 'active' = wrapper resolves the currently-focused note via a small obsidian eval; no vault/file/path may be supplied.

vault.description: Vault display name. REQUIRED when target_mode === 'specific'; FORBIDDEN when target_mode === 'active'. Resolved via the project's lazy vault registry. Unknown vault surfaces CLI_REPORTED_ERROR + details.code: VAULT_NOT_FOUND.

file.description: Vault-relative file path. Alternative to 'path'; exactly one of {file, path} required when target_mode === 'specific'. Subject to the project's structural path-safety refinement (no '../', no leading '/' or '\\', no drive-letter prefix, no control characters). Layer 2 canonical-path check (post-validation) rejects symlink-escape with PATH_ESCAPES_VAULT.

path.description: Vault-relative file path. Alternative to 'file'; exactly one of {file, path} required when target_mode === 'specific'. Same constraints as 'file'.

block_id.description: Block-reference identifier (the bare id, NOT including the leading '^' delimiter). MUST match alphanumeric + hyphen-minus per FR-004 (Obsidian's documented block-id alphabet). MUST NOT begin with '^' — the caret is the wikilink delimiter, not part of the identifier. Length cap is 1000 UTF-16 code units (cohort parity with BI-033 / BI-038 / BI-039 / BI-040). Validation rejects with VALIDATION_ERROR + details.code: INVALID_BLOCK_ID + details.reason in {empty, contains-invalid-chars, leading-caret, too-long}. Case-sensitive throughout (consistent with Obsidian's own treatment). When the same id appears more than once in the same note, first-match-wins in document order per FR-002a (cohort parity with patch_heading FR-006).

content.description: The replacement body for the targeted block. Single placement mode (replace per FR-007); empty content is accepted as the legitimate 'clear the body' operation (cohort parity with patch_heading FR-018a's replace-empty acceptance). Multi-line content into a single-line block shape (list item, table row) is accepted; structural validity of the resulting markdown is the caller's responsibility (the wrapper does not invent shape-specific structural validation).
